reason: Mis-attributed provenance for 'Phi is real-linear (GT-positive-unital)'. The external GT-positive-unital (Idel 2013, refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md Ch.2) does NOT assert linearity in its stated content (it gives only order-unit-norm contractivity, positivity, unitality). Idel's actual linearity definition (refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md:331) is COMPLEX-linearity on the FULL algebra M_d: T(lambda A+B)=lambda T(A)+T(B) for lambda in C; and 'positive map' (Def 2.1, lines 345-347) is defined on M_d, not on B(H)_sa. The real-linearity of Phi on B(H)_sa is in fact a DEFINITIONAL hypothesis of this very proof (def positive-unital-map: 'Phi: B(H)_sa -> B(H)_sa is real-linear, positive, unital'), NOT a theorem derivable from the cited GT source. The cited locus does not contain the claimed fact; correct citation is the def positive-unital-map, not GT-positive-unital. The two are not interchangeable: complex-linearity on M_d does not even type-match real-linearity on B(H)_sa.
